reader_value: Cobalt can remain in the comparison but the observed gap is small, so precision matters before interpreting it.
remaining_gap: Small gap means rounding, denominator, and source-traceability uncertainty can dominate interpretation.
allowed_claim_type: May retain the V2.4 public snapshot comparison with caveats, but should not strengthen the claim until source traceability is added.
claim_boundary: Retain only as caveated public-snapshot comparison; no strengthened claim.
next_action: Defer unless small-gap interpretation becomes important.

reader_value: Lithium is the strongest midstream example because the existing processing/refining top-three value is source-traceable after V2.8.
remaining_gap: Mining baseline and IEA processing/refining denominator should remain clearly separated; IEA mining top-three context is not automatically a replacement for the project mining baseline.
allowed_claim_type: May describe a bounded descriptive mining-versus-processing/refining concentration comparison for the existing lithium V2.4 value.
claim_boundary: Bounded descriptive comparison allowed; no bottleneck proof, risk ranking, investment signal, policy recommendation, geopolitical ranking, or forecast.
next_action: No immediate lithium traceability repair needed.

reader_value: Manganese remains analytically useful as a high-gap case, but only as a downstream battery-material proxy.
remaining_gap: Battery-grade or high-purity manganese sulphate is not automatically equivalent to general manganese processing/refining.
allowed_claim_type: May describe manganese as a downstream battery-material proxy with explicit product-definition and denominator limitations.
claim_boundary: Proxy-limited claim only; no denominator-equivalent processing/refining claim without stronger source alignment.
next_action: Only search denominator-aligned manganese evidence if a stronger manganese claim is needed.

reader_value: Natural graphite is a useful midstream case because it illustrates product-definition sensitivity across mining, flake graphite, spherical graphite, synthetic graphite, coated spherical graphite, and anode material.
remaining_gap: Existing value 99 is not replaced; IEA/Data Explorer or report-table verification is still needed before numeric strengthening.
allowed_claim_type: May retain the V2.4 public snapshot comparison with caveats, but should not strengthen the claim until source traceability is added.
claim_boundary: Public-snapshot only; no claim that natural graphite mining equals spherical graphite processing or that all graphite/anode-material denominators are interchangeable.
next_action: Manual IEA 2025 Data Explorer or table verification is the next step if the 99 value must be hardened.
